Name: Malka Mari National Park
record.name: Malka Mari National Park
record.description: Tucked away in Kenya’s remote northern frontier, Malka Mari National Park is a wilderness gem offering rewarding wildlife safaris, breathtaking scenery, and immersive adventure experiences. Gazetted in 1989, the 876 km² park lies along the Daua River on the Kenya–Ethiopia border in Mandera County, where semi-arid plains meet palm-lined riverine woodlands. Rich in biodiversity and plant endemism, Malka Mari serves as a vital dry-season refuge for wildlife—while providing serene settings for wellness safaris, night wildlife encounters under star-filled skies, and striking landscapes ideal for visual storytelling. Deeply connected to local heritage and shaped by frontier cultures, the park also supports outdoor challenges and group experiences—making it a hidden destination where adventure, conservation, and culture come together.
record.attractions[1]: Daua River Ecosystem: Abundant crocodiles, hippos, and diverse fish species
record.attractions[2]: Unique Plant Endemism:Rare and endemic plant species including Delonix elata, Terminalia, Euphorbia, Acacia, Mellia vulcansii, Aristida, Chloris (foxtail grass), Commiphora, Combretum, Aloe, and Sansevieria
record.attractions[3]: Scenic & Historic Landmarks;  Striking semi-arid landscapes as well as Old colonial military graves, historic buildings, the Goro Ade ruin
record.attractions[4]: Rhamu Airstrip
record.attractions[5]: Takaba airstrip
record.attractions[6]: Malka Mari Airstrip
record.attractions[7]: Mandera Main Airstrip
record.attractions[8]: Elwak Airstrip
record.attractions[9]: Personal effect
record.attractions[10]: Comfortable walking shoes
record.attractions[11]: A hat
